title: ranunculus auricomus subsp. valdesii
intro: Ranunculus auricomus subsp. valdesii, also known as the gold-scaled buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it a potential treatment for various ailments such as wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Ranunculus auricomus subsp. valdesii include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be administered topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
